title: Express 5 Resource Design, Middleware Order, Async Errors, and Cancellation · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds layered Express 5 feed-reader API from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of layered Express 5 feed-reader API while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Design sources, subscriptions, entries, timelines, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions and method semantics.
Order authority, request identity, limits, authentication, negotiation, validation, authorization, handlers, not-found, and error middleware explicitly.
Use Express 5 Promise rejection flow while preventing double next, double send, partial response, and lost unknown causes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Api Resource Decisio…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6001 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile resource decisions, middleware order, async errors, request abort, and response finality; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Api Resource Decisio….
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design sources, subscriptions, entries, timelines, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions and method semantics. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Api Resource Decisio…, keep Feed Ts Api Resource Decisions connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
